title: best herbal teas for ear infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with ear infection because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial or viral infections in the ear. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by the infection. Additionally, drinking chamomile tea can help boost the immune system, allowing the body to better fight off the infection. However, it's important to note that while chamomile tea can provide some relief, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially for severe or persistent ear infections. Lastly, chamomile tea can be used as a complementary therapy, it can be used as a natural remedy to soothe symptoms and promote faster healing.
remedy_recipe: To make chamomile tea for an ear infection, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers (or 2 tea bags) to a teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes, covered, to extract the beneficial compounds and achieve a strong enough infusion.
Strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers, and let it cool down to a warm temperature, ensuring it's not too hot to avoid burns when applying it near the ear.

When the tea has reached a comfortable temperature, soak a clean washcloth in the tea, wring it out, and place it over the infected ear for 5-10 minutes, allowing the chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties to take effect.

Repeat this process 2-3 times a day, discarding the used chamomile and making fresh tea for each application, until symptoms improve. Remember to consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula tea helps with ear infection because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria or virus causing the infection. The tea can also help relieve pain and congestion in the Eustachian tubes, which can contribute to ear infections. Additionally, drinking calendula tea can boost the immune system, helping the body to fight off the infection more effectively. It's important to note that while calendula tea can be a helpful addition to treatment, it should not be used as a substitute for medical attention, especially for severe or persistent ear infections. Lastly, the tea can be used as a warm compress, which can provide soothing relief to the affected ear.
remedy_recipe: To make calendula tea for ear infection, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried calendula flowers, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers, then let it cool down to a warm temperature that is comfortable to the touch.
Fill a clean dropper with the calendula tea, ensuring that it is not too hot.
Tilt your head to the side and gently squeeze a few drops of the tea into the affected ear, keeping your head tilted for a few minutes to allow the tea to penetrate the ear.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a day until symptoms improve, making sure to discard any remaining tea after each use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea tea helps with ear infection because it has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The tea is made from the leaves and flowers of the echinacea plant, which contain phenols, volatile oils, and flavonoids that have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral effects. These compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the ears and alleviate the pain and pressure associated with ear infections. Additionally, echinacea tea can help to boost the body's overall immune response, making it easier for the body to fight off infections and recover more quickly. However, it's important to note that echinacea tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, and anyone experiencing symptoms of an ear infection should consult a healthcare professional.
remedy_recipe: To make echinacea tea for an ear infection, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 2 teaspoons of dried echinacea flowers and/or leaves; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the plant material, then add the juice of half a lemon and 1-2 teaspoons of honey (optional) for taste and additional antimicrobial benefits.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature, ensuring it's not too hot to cause discomfort when applying it to the ear.
Soak a clean cotton ball in the echinacea tea and gently squeeze out any excess liquid.

Tilt your head to the side and place the damp cotton ball in the ear for 5-10 minutes, allowing the liquid to slowly enter the ear canal and providing relief and potential antibacterial properties. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tea helps with ear infection because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can reduce inflammation and fight the bacterial infection in the ear. The tea is made from the goldenseal herb, which contains berberine, a compound known to be effective in treating various types of infections. Drinking goldenseal tea may also help boost the immune system, enabling the body to fight off the infection more effectively. However, it is important to note that goldenseal tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, but rather as a complementary therapy. As with any natural remedy, it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before use.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, bring 1 cup of water to a boil, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried goldenseal root, reduce heat to low, cover and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, let the mixture cool down to a comfortable temperature, then strain the liquid to remove the goldenseal pieces.
Fill a clean dropper bottle with the cooled goldenseal tea, and lie down on your side with the affected ear facing up.
Gently squeeze the dropper to release 2-3 drops of the goldenseal tea into the ear, allowing it to sit in the ear for 5-10 minutes.
After 5-10 minutes, sit up and tilt your head to drain the liquid, then clean any remaining liquid with a tissue or cloth. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for 3-4 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavender tea helps with ear infection because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate the pain and swelling associated with ear infections. The soothing effects of lavender can also help reduce inflammation in the Eustachian tubes, which can become blocked and lead to infection. Additionally, lavender has natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help fight off the bacteria or virus causing the infection. Drinking lavender tea can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can help boost the immune system and speed up the healing process. However, it is important to note that while lavender tea may provide relief for mild ear infections, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially for severe or chronic ear infections.
remedy_recipe: To make lavender tea for ear infection, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried culinary lavender flowers; let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid to remove the flowers.
Allow the lavender tea to cool down to a warm temperature, ensuring it's not too hot to avoid causing discomfort when used near the ear.
Mix in 1-2 teaspoons of raw honey into the lukewarm lavender tea, stirring until it's fully dissolved, to help soothe any irritation and potentially enhance the therapeutic effects.
Tilt your head to the side and gently pour or use a syringe to apply the lavender tea into the affected ear, being careful not to let it get too deep into the ear canal; let it sit for 5-10 minutes.

After the lavender tea has had time to soak in the ear, tilt your head to allow the liquid to drain, then gently dry the ear with a clean cloth or tissue; repeat this process 2-3 times a day for 3-5 days for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with ear infection because it has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the ear. The warm nature of ginger tea can also help to alleviate pressure build-up in the Eustachian tubes, which can cause discomfort in the ear. Additionally, ginger tea contains compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which have antimicrobial and antibacterial effects that can help to fight against the bacterial or viral infections causing the ear infection. Furthermore, ginger tea can help to boost the immune system, which can speed up the healing process and prevent the infection from spreading. Lastly, ginger tea is a natural and gentle alternative to over-the-counter medications, making it a safe and effective remedy for both children and adults.
remedy_recipe: Peel and finely chop 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger, then add it to a pot with 2 cups of water; bring the water to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and 1/4 teaspoon of lemon juice; allow the mixture to cool down to a safe temperature.
Strain the ginger tea into a glass, discarding the solids.
Tilt your head to one side and, using a spoon, slowly pour a small amount of the ginger tea into the affected ear; let it sit for 5-10 minutes, then tilt your head to drain the liquid.
Repeat steps 4 and 5 with the other ear if needed, then discard any remaining tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mullein tea helps with ear infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate the pain and swelling associated with ear infections. Additionally, mullein has antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help fight off the infection-causing pathogens. The tea can also help reduce inflammation in the Eustachian tubes, which can become inflamed and blocked during an ear infection. Furthermore, mullein tea can help soothe the mucous membranes in the ear, providing relief from the discomfort of an ear infection. Finally, mullein tea is a natural and gentle remedy that can be used in conjunction with other treatments or on its own to help alleviate the symptoms of ear infections.
remedy_recipe: To make mullein tea for ear infection, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried mullein flowers and leaves; let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
After steeping, strain the tea to remove the mullein parts, then allow the liquid to cool down to a warm temperature.
Once warm, take a clean dropper and fill it with the mullein tea.
Tilt your head to the side and gently drop 2-3 drops of the tea into the affected ear, being careful to avoid touching the dropper to the ear.
Keep your head tilted for a few minutes to allow the tea to penetrate the ear canal, then repeat the process 2-3 times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Garlic tea helps with ear infection because of its natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the compound allicin found in garlic. This powerful compound can help to fight off the bacteria or viruses causing the ear infection, while its anti-inflammatory effects can alleviate pain and swelling in the Eustachian tubes and middle ear. Furthermore, garlic tea's warmth can help to soothe the ear and provide additional relief, making it an effective and comforting home remedy for ear infections. It is important to note that while garlic tea can be beneficial, it should not replace medical treatment, and individuals should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and guidance. Lastly, garlic tea can be prepared easily by steeping crushed garlic in hot water, allowing the active compounds to infuse, and then straining the liquid before consumption.
remedy_recipe: Peel and finely mince 3 cloves of garlic, then let it sit for 10 minutes to activate its health-promoting compounds (this step is important, as it allows the allicin in garlic to form, which has antibacterial and antiviral properties); bring 2 cups of water to a boil in a small saucepan, then add the minced garlic and reduce heat to low; let it simmer for 15 minutes.
After simmering, strain the garlic-infused water into a mug, discarding the garlic pieces; squeeze half of a lemon into the garlic water for its vitamin C and additional antimicrobial benefits.
Peel and crush a small piece of ginger (about 1 inch long), adding it to the garlic-lemon water for its anti-inflammatory properties and spicy flavor.

Warm 1 tablespoon of honey in a small dish in the microwave for 10-15 seconds, then add it to the garlic-lemon-ginger tea, stirring until it's fully dissolved; raw honey adds soothing properties and a touch of natural sweetness.

Sip the garlic tea warm, taking breaks as needed; aim to drink the whole mug, as the garlic-ginger-lemon-honey combination can help alleviate ear infection symptoms and support your immune system. Enjoy!

remedies.remedy_desc: Saint-John's-wort tea helps with ear infection because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate the pain and swelling associated with ear infections. Additionally, it contains hypericin and hyperforin, which have been found to have antibacterial and antiviral effects, potentially helping to combat the underlying infection. The tea may also help to improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications. However, it's important to note that while Saint-John's-wort tea may provide some relief, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in cases of severe or chronic ear infections. As with any natural remedy, it's always best to consult with a healthcare provider before use.
remedy_recipe: To make Saint-John's-Wort tea for ear infection, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 2 teaspoons of dried Saint-John's-Wort flowers and leaves; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the plant material and allow it to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
While waiting for the tea to cool, prepare a clean dropper by boiling it in water for 5 minutes and letting it dry.
Once the tea is cooled, use the dropper to fill your ear with the Saint-John's-Wort tea, being careful to not let the liquid go deeper than your eardrum.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a day, making a fresh batch of tea each time, until symptoms subside.

remedies.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with ear infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the ear. Its antimicrobial and antibacterial components also help fight the infection-causing bacteria or viruses. Furthermore, yarrow tea's natural astringent properties can help dry up excess fluid in the ear, which can create a conducive environment for bacterial growth. Additionally, yarrow tea's antioxidant properties can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the infection more effectively. However, it is important to note that while yarrow tea can provide relief and support the healing process, it should not replace medical treatment, especially for severe or persistent ear infections.
remedy_recipe: To make yarrow tea for ear infection, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers or 4 teaspoons of fresh yarrow flowers to a teapot.
Allow the water to steep with the yarrow flowers for 10-15 minutes before straining the mixture to remove the solid plant material.
While the tea is still warm, soak a clean washcloth in the yarrow tea and squeeze out any excess liquid.
Place the warm, wet washcloth over the affected ear for 5-10 minutes, reheating the washcloth as needed to maintain a comfortable temperature.
Discard any remaining tea and repeat the process 2-3 times daily for best results, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Olive leaf tea helps with ear infection because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight the bacteria or viruses causing the infection. Its active compound, oleuropein, has been shown to have antibacterial effects against certain strains of bacteria that can cause ear infections. Additionally, olive leaf tea can help boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the infection more effectively. The anti-inflammatory properties of olive leaf tea may also help relieve pain and discomfort associated with ear infections. However, it's important to note that while olive leaf tea may help alleviate symptoms and support recovery, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for ear infections.
remedy_recipe: To make olive leaf tea for an ear infection, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried olive leaves (or 2-3 fresh olive leaves) to a teapot.
Allow the water to steep with the olive leaves for 5-10 minutes, covered, to extract the beneficial compounds and achieve a strong tea.
After steeping, strain the tea to remove the leaves and retain the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature, ideally close to body temperature, to avoid any discomfort or harm to the ears.
Once cooled, use a dropper to apply 2-3 drops of the olive leaf tea into the affected ear, twice a day, for 3-5 days, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Clove tea helps with ear infection because it has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to fight the infection and reduce pain. The main active compound in cloves, eugenol, has been shown to be effective against bacteria and viruses that can cause ear infections. Drinking clove tea can also help to reduce inflammation in the ear and surrounding tissues, providing relief from symptoms such as pressure and discomfort. Additionally, clove tea can help to boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. However, it's important to note that while clove tea can help to alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for a severe ear infection.
remedy_recipe: To make clove tea for ear infection, crush 10 whole cloves using a mortar and pestle, then place the crushed cloves in a pot and add 2 cups of water; bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
After simmering, strain the clove tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a mug, discarding the solid clove pieces.
Allow the clove tea to cool down to a safe temperature, ensuring it's warm but not hot to the touch.

Fill a small dropper bottle with the warm clove tea, then tilt your head to the side and gently squeeze a few drops of the clove tea into the affected ear, being careful not to let the dropper touch the ear.
Keep your head tilted for a few minutes to allow the clove tea to penetrate the ear canal, then repeat the process 2-3 times a day until symptoms subside.

remedies.remedy_desc: Tea tree tea helps with ear infection because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight the bacteria causing the infection. The tea tree oil found in the tea can also help to alleviate pain and discomfort. Moreover, its antimicrobial properties can aid in preventing the spread of the infection to other parts of the ear or to other people. However, it is important to note that tea tree tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, but rather as a complementary remedy. It is always best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedy_recipe: Combine 1 cup of boiling water with 1-2 teaspoons of dried tea tree leaves, allowing the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to create a strong tea tree tea.
Strain the tea to remove leaves, resulting in tea tree tea, and let it cool down to a warm temperature that is comfortable to touch.
Soak a clean cotton ball in the cooled tea tree tea, ensuring it is not too hot to avoid burning the sensitive skin inside the ear.
Tilt the affected ear upward and gently place the soaked cotton ball on the outer ear, allowing the tea tree tea to slowly penetrate the ear canal.
Keep the soaked cotton ball in place for 5-10 minutes, then remove and discard, repeating this process 2-3 times daily for 3-4 days to help alleviate ear infection symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Basil tea helps with ear infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight the bacterial infection in the ear. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to alleviate pressure and pain in the ear. Additionally, basil tea is rich in antioxidants that can help strengthen the immune system and promote faster healing. Furthermore, basil tea has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat ear infections and other respiratory conditions. However, it is important to note that while basil tea can be a helpful addition to a treatment plan, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in consultation with a healthcare provider.
remedy_recipe: To make basil tea for ear infection, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1 teaspoon of dried basil leaves; let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the basil leaves, then add honey or sugar to taste while the tea is still warm.
Allow the tea to cool down to a safe temperature, ensuring it's warm but not hot.
Using a dropper, put 2-3 drops of the basil tea into the affected ear, being careful to avoid touching the ear with the dropper.
Keep the tea in the ear for 5-10 minutes, then let it drain out by tilting your head to the side. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for 2-3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cayenne tea helps with ear infection because it contains capsaicin, a compound with natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. This substance can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the Eustachian tubes and middle ear, providing relief from infection symptoms. Furthermore, cayenne tea's warmth can alleviate pressure build-up caused by fluid accumulation in the ear, promoting drainage and healing. Additionally, the immune-boosting qualities of cayenne pepper may strengthen the body's defenses against infection, potentially shortening the duration of earaches. However, it is essential to note that while cayenne tea can provide temporary relief, it should not replace medical treatment for ear infections, especially if they are severe or persistent.
remedy_recipe: To make cayenne tea for ear infection, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1/4-1/2 teaspoon of cayenne pepper, depending on desired spiciness, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a mug, discarding the pepper, and let it cool down to a safe temperature, about 10-15 minutes.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste for flavor and additional soothing properties.
Tilt your head to the side and use a dropper to gently squeeze 2-3 drops of the tea into the affected ear.
Keep your head tilted for a few minutes to allow the tea to penetrate the ear canal, then drain by sitting up and letting any excess liquid drain out.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cat's claw tea helps with ear infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria or viruses causing the infection. The tea is made from the bark of the Uncaria tomentosa vine, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including ear infections. The antioxidants in cat's claw tea can also help boost the immune system, allowing the body to fight off the infection more effectively. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with ear infections. However, it is important to note that cat's claw tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_recipe: To make cat's claw tea for ear infection, bring 1 cup of water to a boil, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried cat's claw bark and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, remove the tea from heat and let it cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the cat's claw bark and discard the solid remains.
For ear drops, let the tea cool down to room temperature, then use a dropper to apply a few drops of the tea into the affected ear.
For a soothing compress, soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea, then apply the cloth to the outside of the ear for 10-15 minutes, 3-4 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with ear infection because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight the bacteria causing the infection. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to alleviate pain and discomfort in the ear. Additionally, fennel is known to have a natural ability to reduce fluid buildup, which can be beneficial in treating ear infections. Furthermore, fennel tea can help to relax muscles and reduce tension, which can be helpful in relieving the pressure and discomfort associated with ear infections. Finally, fennel tea is a natural and gentle alternative to over-the-counter or prescription medications, making it a safe option for both children and adults.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2 teaspoons of fennel seeds using a mortar and pestle, then place them in a pot and add 1 cup of water; bring it to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot and let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow the water to absorb the fennel's medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a mug, discarding the spent fennel seeds, then let it cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Add a little honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired, for improved flavor and additional health benefits.
Sip the fennel tea slowly, allowing it to reach your ears as you swallow, providing soothing relief for ear infection discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Marshmallow root tea helps with ear infection because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the ear. The tea's demulcent quality coats and soothes the mucous membranes of the ears, providing relief from irritation and infection. Additionally, marshmallow root contains mucilage, a sticky substance that helps to remove excess wax and debris from the ears, promoting better ear health. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties can help to combat the bacteria or viruses causing the ear infection. However, it is important to note that marshmallow root tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with professional medical advice.
remedy_recipe: To make marshmallow root tea for ear infection, bring 1 quart of water to a boil, then add 2-3 teaspoons of dried marshmallow root and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the marshmallow root pieces, allowing the tea to cool down to a warm temperature.
As the tea cools, soak a clean cotton ball in the tea and squeeze out any excess liquid.
Carefully place the damp cotton ball in the affected ear, ensuring it covers the ear canal opening, and leave it in place for 5-10 minutes.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a day, discarding the used cotton ball and making fresh tea each time, to help alleviate ear infection symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Red clover tea helps with ear infection because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight the bacteria causing the infection. The isopropanolic alkaloid present in red clover tea, known as formononetin, is particularly effective in inhibiting the growth of bacteria responsible for ear infections. Additionally, red clover tea's antioxidant properties can help boost the immune system, which is crucial in fighting off infections. Furthermore, red clover tea's soothing effect can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with ear infections. However, it is important to note that red clover tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with a healthcare professional's advice.
remedy_recipe: To make red clover tea for ear infection, start by boiling 2 cups of water and adding 2 teaspoons of dried red clover flowers; let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers, allowing it to cool down to a comfortable warm temperature.
Soak a clean washcloth in the red clover tea and wring it out gently.
Place the warm, wet washcloth over the affected ear for 5-10 minutes, ensuring it's not too hot to avoid burns.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a day, discarding any leftover tea after 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with ear infection because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight the bacteria causing the infection. Its natural antimicrobial properties can help to alleviate pain and discomfort, while its soothing effects can help to reduce swelling and promote healing. Additionally, sage tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have a positive impact on overall ear health. By drinking sage tea or using it as a compress, the healing compounds can be directly applied to the affected area, providing quicker and more effective relief. However, it is important to note that while sage tea can be a helpful addition to traditional treatments, it should not be used as a substitute for medical advice or treatment from a healthcare professional.
remedy_recipe: To make sage tea for an ear infection, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of fresh sage leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried sage (adjust to taste); allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the sage leaves, then let it cool down to a safe temperature, making sure it's warm, but not hot, to the touch.
Fill a clean dropper with the sage tea, and gently squeeze a few drops into the affected ear, being careful to avoid touching the ear with the dropper.
Allow the sage tea to sit in the ear for 5-10 minutes, then tilt the head to drain the liquid, and gently pat the ear dry with a clean cloth or tissue.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional, for relief from ear infection symptoms.
